name: Astur-Galaica Road Loop
name_original: Playa de Porcía a Tapia de Casariego — circular por Reiriz
description: This difficult 18.5-mile touring bicycle loop in Asturias and Galicia offers challenging climbs and coastal views, passing Porcía Beach.
region_intro: Touring cycling around El Franco, situated in Asturias, Spain, offers a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region is characterized by a blend of rugged coastline and rolling hills, providing varied terrain. Cyclists can expect routes that traverse coastal paths with sea views and inland trails with moderate elevation changes. The area's natural features include beaches like Porcía and Penarronda, alongside picturesque fishing villages. Best touring cycling routes around El Franco The most popular touring cycling route is Astur-Galaica Road Loop, a 18.5 miles (29.8 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and passes by Porcía Beach. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Hill view down to Navia – Tapia de Casariego loop from Tapia de Casariego, a difficult 39.9 miles (64.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation gains and challenging climbs. Local touring cyclists also love the Tapia de Casariego – View of the Peirao de Porcillán loop from Tapia de Casariego, a 26.3 miles (42.4 km) trail leading through coastal areas and connecting to charming fishing villages, often completed in about 2 hours 40 minutes. Touring cycling around El Franco is defined by its coastline, rolling hills, and access to fishing villages. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with considerable climbs. The routes in El Franco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore El Franco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in El Franco?
A: El Franco offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 60 different tours to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in El Franco?
A: The terrain in El Franco is diverse, characterized by a blend of rugged coastline and rolling hills. You can expect routes that traverse scenic coastal paths with refreshing sea views, as well as inland trails featuring moderate to significant elevation changes and challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in El Franco?
A: While many routes in El Franco offer a challenge, there are a few easier options available. For instance, the region has 4 routes classified as easy, providing a gentler introduction to touring cycling in the area.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in El Franco?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes in El Franco are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33807019" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapia de Casariego – View of the Peirao de Porcillán loop from Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 42.4 km trail.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I can see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Along the touring cycling routes in El Franco, you can discover several natural features and landmarks. Highlights include the unique shell-shaped &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penarronda Beach&lt;/a&gt;, known for its rare flora, and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porcía Beach&lt;/a&gt;. You might also encounter viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5561255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta de la Atalaya&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5647078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Cañones and Natural Pool of Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any beaches accessible by touring bike in El Franco?
A: Absolutely. El Franco's coastal location means many touring cycling routes offer access to beautiful beaches. You can cycle past or stop at &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353374" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Porcía Beach&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359293" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Penarronda Beach&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/368078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Serantes Beach&lt;/a&gt;, enjoying the refreshing sea breezes and coastal vistas.

Q: What do other cyclists say about touring cycling in El Franco?
A: The touring cycling routes in El Franco are highly rated by the komoot community, with an impressive average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 600 touring cyclists have already explored the varied terrain, often praising the stunning coastal views and the diverse challenges offered by the routes.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of touring cycling routes in El Franco?
A: The routes vary significantly in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1029987614/astur-galaica-road-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Astur-Galaica Road Loop&lt;/a&gt; is about 29.8 km (18.5 miles) and takes around 2 hours 5 minutes, while a more challenging route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40360619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hill view down to Navia – Tapia de Casariego loop from Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt; spans 64.1 km (39.9 miles) and can take over 4 hours.

Q: Are there options for challenging touring cycling routes with significant climbs?
A: Yes, for experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge, El Franco offers routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40360619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hill view down to Navia – Tapia de Casariego loop from Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a difficult 64.1 km path featuring considerable climbs, making it suitable for those looking for a strenuous ride.

Q: Is El Franco a good destination for touring cyclists who enjoy coastal views?
A: El Franco is an excellent destination for coastal touring cycling. Its location in Asturias provides stunning coastal views, with routes often traversing paths that offer refreshing sea breezes and picturesque vistas of the Cantabrian Sea. Many routes pass by beautiful beaches and charming fishing villages.

Q: Are there any charming villages or towns to visit along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, the touring cycling routes in El Franco often connect to charming villages and towns. &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/353378" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tapia de Casariego&lt;/a&gt; is a notable fishing village with a pleasant promenade and port, offering an attractive stop for cyclists. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/363278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navia&lt;/a&gt; is also accessible via some routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in El Franco?
A: While specific weather data isn't provided, Asturias generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling from spring through autumn. The coastal location means milder temperatures, but it's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before your trip, especially for wind and rain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling options in El Franco?
A: While many routes in El Franco are moderate to difficult, there are a few easier options that might be more suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. It's recommended to check the difficulty ratings and elevation profiles of individual routes to find the best fit for your family's abilities.

Q: What facilities or amenities can I expect along the routes, such as places to stop for refreshments?
A: The routes often pass through or near charming fishing villages and towns like Tapia de Casariego and Navia, where you can find various amenities. These typically include cafes, restaurants, and shops, providing opportunities for refreshments and breaks during your ride. Some beaches, like Penarronda, also offer facilities such as beach bars.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Astur-Galaica Road Loop?
faqs.answer: The Astur-Galaica Road Loop begins near Playa de Porcía. You can typically find parking options in the vicinity of the beach or in the nearby town of Tapia de Casariego, which is also a key highlight on the route.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Astur-Galaica Road Loop for touring cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. The Astur-Galaica region is renowned for its significant mountainous terrain, featuring "huge climbs, deep valleys," and very little flat riding. Cyclists should be prepared for winding ascents and demanding gradients, characteristic of the cycling in Asturias and Galicia.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle the Astur-Galaica Road Loop is generally during the spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October) months. During these periods, the weather is typically more stable and pleasant for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter.

faqs.question: Is the Astur-Galaica Road Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the route itself is a road cycling loop, the general region of Asturias and Galicia is known for its natural beauty. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they are well-behaved and kept on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near livestock. Always check local regulations for specific areas or beaches you might visit off-route.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or is there an entrance fee for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are required to cycle the Astur-Galaica Road Loop itself. However, a significant portion of the route passes through the Zona Húmida Protexida Ría de Ribadeo. While cycling on public roads within this protected area is generally permitted, always respect local regulations regarding wildlife, litter, and noise, especially in sensitive ecological zones.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and landmarks can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking and diverse scenery, combining coastal views with lush green landscapes. Key highlights include Porcía Beach, the charming settlement of Tapia de Casariego, and stunning vistas from viewpoints like View of Puerto de Tapia de Casariego from Mirador de La Guardia and Los Cañones Viewpoint. You'll experience a mix of wild coasts and rolling green valleys.

faqs.question: Does the Astur-Galaica Road Loop intersect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Astur-Galaica Road Loop shares sections with several other routes. Notably, 42% of it follows the 'Road Astur-Galaica', 22% is part of 'Etapa 27 Viavélez - Tapia de Casariego', and 12% aligns with the 'Northern Route (Asturia)'. Smaller portions also intersect with 'Mtb Northern Route (Asturia)', 'Road Oscos, Cuenca del Navia y Sierra de Penouta', 'Etapa 28 Senda Costera Tapia de Casariego - Vegadeo', and 'Senda Costera Tapia de Casariego - Figueras'.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or find accommodation along the Astur-Galaica Road Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near several small villages and towns, most notably Tapia de Casariego. These settlements offer opportunities to find local gastronomy, traditional bars, and potentially accommodation. It's advisable to check opening hours, especially outside of peak season.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain should I expect on this touring bicycle route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly paved roads, but given the region's mountainous character, expect significant elevation changes. You'll encounter winding roads, steep ascents, and descents. While roads are generally well-maintained, the constant undulations mean there's hardly any flat riding, requiring good fitness and appropriate gearing.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner touring cyclists or families?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating and the significant elevation gain (475m over 29.8km), this route is not recommended for beginner touring cyclists or families with young children. The challenging terrain and demanding climbs are better suited for experienced cyclists looking for a strenuous ride.

faqs.question: How long should I plan for the Astur-Galaica Road Loop?
faqs.answer: The estimated cycling duration for this 29.8 km loop is approximately 2 hours and 5 minutes. However, this is a pure riding time estimate. To fully enjoy the scenic viewpoints, explore Tapia de Casariego, and take breaks, you should plan for a half-day excursion.
